verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is primarily an ER-resident single-pass type I transmembrane protein whose canonical function (SOCE via Orai1 activation) occurs while it remains anchored in the ER membrane at ER-PM contact sites — it does not insert into the PM during store-operated signaling. However, a documented minority pool (STIM1PM) is genuinely integrated into the plasma membrane as a type I TM protein with an extracellular N-terminus in certain cell types (e.g., pancreatic epithelial Panc-1 cells), stabilized by N-glycosylation at N131 and N171. This surface pool is cell-type restricted and not the dominant localization. Because STIM1PM reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM domain (not via recruitment by another surface protein), the verdict is contextual rather than no.
